query: Run the rnaseq pipeline version 1.7 on sample Liver_RNA_1 and tell me when it finishes.
expected_behavior: Calls GET /me first and confirms `id` is non-null before running.
Discovers the pipeline (GET /pipelines), then resolves the named version '1.7' via GET /pipelines/<pipeline_id> by matching `versions[].name` rather than defaulting to the most recent.
Fetches GET /pipelines/versions/<version_id> for the schema and resolves the sample by name to an id.
Builds a valid run body (params/data_params/csv_params per schema type) and shows a confirmation summary before the mutating POST.

On confirmation submits a single curl POST to /pipelines/versions/<version_id>/run with the auth header and `-A "flow-ai/<version>"`, and reports the execution id, identifier, status, and the UI link.

Because the user asked to be told when it finishes, then polls GET /executions/<id> on an interval of at least 60 seconds (runs can take hours) until `status` reaches a terminal value (OK, ERROR, or CANCELED).
Does NOT poll faster than ~once per minute and does NOT busy-wait; a long interval is expected and acceptable.
On terminal OK reports success with the final status and may surface a short log tail. On ERROR surfaces the execution's stderr/log rather than claiming success.
Carries `Authorization: Bearer …` on every request, including each poll.
